---
title: "Baba, Department of Social Work, Niigata University of Health and Welfare"
---

The laboratory (zemi) conducts practical research focusing on community development and community-oriented care. Our main areas of study are within Niigata Prefecture and Tokyo.

In Japan’s long-term care insurance system, alongside residential care and home-based care, there is also community-oriented care. In community-oriented care, it is believed that the quality of life (QOL) of service users is maintained and improved by visiting various locations within the local community—such as their own homes, day care centres, local supermarkets, convenience stores, public bathhouses, and friends’ homes. Community-based care includes dementia group homes and small-scale multi-functional home care services.

In addition to undergraduate students, we welcome people with lived experience, students and postgraduates from other universities, international researchers, and secondary school students.

## Members

- Yoshihiko Baba [ORCID](https://orcid.org/0000-0001-7034-9724)

## Practice Educator Network

The network of long-term care practice trainers.

## Links

- Niigata University of Health and Welfare
  - [Department of Social Welfare](https://www.nuhw.ac.jp/faculty/sw/)
- Niigata University
  - [Matsui Lab., Architecture Program](https://matsui2014.wixsite.com/urbandesignlab)
